probes.name: sports-registry-tables-migrated
probes.description: The `sports` + `disciplines` + `positions` tables exist + carry the latest schema version. Failure indicates a missed migration — every Wave 3 module depends on this schema.

probes.name: sports-registry-platform-catalog-loaded
probes.description: The platform-seeded catalog is present (SELECT COUNT WHERE tenant_id IS NULL >= 30). Failure means SeedPlatformSportsCatalogJob hasn't run OR the boot listener failed silently. Every Wave 3 write depends on the catalog being present — this probe is critical.

probes.name: sports-registry-catalog-json-parseable
probes.description: `data/platform-sports-catalog.json` parses cleanly + validates against the ships schema. Failure indicates a corrupted deploy artifact — a reseed would fail.

probes.name: sports-registry-tenant-fk-integrity
probes.description: Every non-null `sports.tenant_id` / `disciplines.tenant_id` / `positions.tenant_id` resolves to a live tenant. FK is CASCADE so orphans should be impossible via the DB; this probe catches direct SQL manipulation.

probes.name: sports-registry-slug-uniqueness
probes.description: No two active rows in the same scoping tuple share a slug. Sport: (tenant_id NULL for platform, slug) OR (tenant_id X, slug) unique. Same for disciplines under (sport_id, slug) + positions under (discipline_id, slug). Backed by partial unique indexes; the probe verifies the indexes are in place + healthy.

probes.name: sports-registry-parent-fk-integrity
probes.description: Every `disciplines.sport_id` resolves to a live sport; every `positions.discipline_id` resolves to a live discipline. FKs are RESTRICT so the DB blocks the deletion; this probe catches direct SQL manipulation.

probes.name: sports-registry-cross-tenant-references
probes.description: SECURITY probe. Verifies that no `disciplines.tenant_id` differs from its parent `sports.tenant_id` in a cross-tenant way (parent must be same-tenant OR platform-seeded). Same for positions vs disciplines. A failure indicates a security anomaly — cross-tenant FK smuggling.

probes.name: sports-registry-resolver-reachable
probes.description: SportKeyResolver responds to a known-good query (`resolve('football', null, null, null)` against the platform catalog). Failure indicates the resolver's cache layer + DB path are both down.

probes.name: sports-registry-reconciler-recent
probes.description: ReconcileSportReferencesJob has completed in the last 26 hours (nightly + 2h grace). Reads from `sports:reconciler_last_run` cache key. Failure indicates the scheduled task is stuck.

probes.name: sports-registry-drift-count-bounded
probes.description: The count of `SportPrefixResolutionFailed` events in the last 24 hours is below `config('sports_registry.health.drift_alarm_threshold')` (default 10). A high count signals downstream schema drift OR a catalog reseed that deactivated a slug still in use — either way, ops attention needed.

probes.name: sports-registry-purge-queue-drained
probes.description: No PurgeArchivedRegistryRowsJob has been pending for > 8 days (weekly + 1d grace). Catches queue stalls that could push deactivated customs indefinitely past their 730-day retention window.
